RE: XXXX. Acquisition Dear Mr.
____________:
The purpose of this letter of intent is to set forth certain general terms of a proposed transaction
between YYYY, a Delaware corporation (“YYYY”), and a wholly-owned subsidiary of or one of
YYYY’s subsidiaries or other affiliates (such entity being referred to herein as “Purchaser”), and
XXXX and its wholly- owned subsidiaries —___________________________(the “Subsidiaries”)
(collectively the “Company” or “XXXX”). The transaction is subject to satisfactory completion by
YYYY of a diligence review of the Company, the approval of the Board of Directors of YYYY, and
the execution and delivery by Purchaser, the Company and the stockholders of the Company (the
“Stockholders”) of a mutually satisfactory purchase agreement (the “Purchase Agreement”).
The principal terms of the proposed transaction would be substantially as follows:
1. 	Real and Effective Acquisition. Purchaser would purchase 100% of the stock and other equity
interests and rights including but not limited to warrants, options, phantom equity and convertible
securities in XXXX (collectively, the “Equity Interests”). At Closing, XXXX would own, except
as otherwise provided in the Purchase Agreement, all of the assets used, held for use or currently
made available to XXXX for use in connection with its businesses, which would include, without
limitation: (i) all assets, including accounts receivable, all electronic parts inventory, other current
assets, tools, all tangible property, including but not limited to computer hardware and software,
vehicles, furniture and fixtures and leasehold improvements, at each business location, (ii) all
customer lists, and (iii) the goodwill associated therewith, all free and clear of any security
interests, mortgages, or other encumbrances.
Sample LOI Stock

-- 1 of 6 --

a. 	Purchase 	Price. 	The 	aggregate 	consideration 	for 	the 	Equity 	Interests 	would 	be
$________________ (payable at the Closing.
2. 	Net Working Capital Adjustment. There will be a final net working capital adjustment after
the closing (the “Closing”) of the transactions contemplated by the Purchase Agreement to address any
change in the actual net working capital of the Company as of the Closing. To accomplish this,
Purchaser and the Company would first agree upon a target net working capital amount, which amount
shall be based on the amount of working capital historically maintained by the Company to operate the
business based on its recent activity levels. We shall then compare the target net working capital
amount to the amount of actual net working capital at Closing and
increase or decrease the purchase price after Closing to take into account any change in the net
working capital. This final adjustment would be completed within 90 days of Closing.
3. 	Purchase Agreement - Escrow. The Purchase Agreement would contain such representations,
warranties, covenants and other agreements (including agreements regarding indemnification) of the
Company, the Stockholders and Purchaser as are customary for transactions of this type or as are
otherwise acceptable to the parties thereto. At Closing, Purchaser would deposit a portion of the
Purchase Price equal to __% of the Purchase Price in an escrow account with a mutually acceptable
escrow agent as partial security for the indemnification obligations of the Stockholders arising under
the Purchase Agreement; it being understood that such liability shall be joint and several and shall not
be limited to the amount of such holdback. Assuming there are no indemnifiable claims then-
outstanding, such escrow amount (or remaining portion thereof) would be released by escrow agent to
Stockholders one (1) year after the Closing.
4. 	Purchaser’s Financing of the Transaction. Purchaser will finance the purchase internally.
OR
There will be no financing contingency.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 6 --

5. 	Due Diligence Review. Promptly following the execution of this letter of intent, the Company
will allow Purchaser to complete Purchaser’s examination of the Company’s financial, accounting,
business record, contracts, and other legal documents to complete due diligence. Any information
obtained by Purchaser as a result thereof will be maintained by Purchaser in confidence subject to the
terms of the Confidentiality Agreement executed by the Company and YYYY. The parties will cooperate
to complete due diligence expeditiously. A list of the requested due diligence documents is attached.
6. 	Definitive Purchase Agreement. All of the terms and conditions of the proposed transaction
would be stated in the Purchase Agreement, to be negotiated, agreed and executed by the Company,
the Stockholders and Purchaser. Neither party intends to be bound by any oral or written statements or
correspondence concerning the Purchase Agreement arising during the course of negotiations,
notwithstanding that the same may be expressed in terms signifying a partial, preliminary or interim
agreement between the parties.
7. 	Conduct of Business in the Ordinary Course. In addition to the conditions discussed in this
letter of intent and others to be contained in the Purchase Agreement, consummation of the transaction
would be subject to the Company having conducted its business in the ordinary course in accordance
with historical practices between the date of this letter of intent and the date of Closing and there having
been no material adverse change in the Company’s business, assets, liabilities, financial condition or
prospects. The Company will use reasonable efforts to maintain its relations with all employees,
suppliers and customers and conduct its business in a proper, prudent and lawful manner in keeping
with its current practices so as to protect its assets, business, goodwill and prospects. The Company
will continue to maintain insurance coverage
on its assets and operations at existing levels. Except for assignments, transfers and other
dispositions in the ordinary course of business, during the Exclusivity Period (defined below),
the Company will not dispose of, assign, transfer or encumber in any manner any of its assets
or resources of the business without the prior written consent of Purchaser.
8. 	Business Plan. Purchaser plans to operate the business of the Company as a business unit of
YYYY and would work with the Company to develop the long term growth, cost efficiencies and
synergies with the Purchaser.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 6 --

9. 	Continuation of Employment. Purchaser intends to cause the Company to continue to
employ, or alternatively offer employment to, substantially all of the Company’s employees, and would
expect the Company to use reasonable efforts to assist Purchaser in retaining, or employing, these
individuals, and will assist Purchaser in its or the Company’s efforts to enter into employment
agreements with certain key employees of the Company, which will include employment provisions
satisfactory to Purchaser and such employees and a continuation of the existing non-compete
restrictions of such key employees. Purchaser agrees to pay change in control bonuses to such
employees as Stockholders designate and the amount of such payments shall be deducted from the
Purchase Price.
10. 	Acceptance and Closing. It is currently anticipated that the Closing would occur on or before
_______________, following the Company’s timely acceptance of this letter of intent, the completion
of Purchaser’s due diligence review and the preparation and negotiation of the Purchase Agreement
and related documents. If this letter of intent is not signed by all parties and received by YYYY before
such time, this offer shall lapse and become null and void and no party shall have any obligation to
any other hereunder.
11. 	Expenses. Purchaser and the Stockholders (on behalf of themselves and the Company) shall
each pay its own expenses, including but not limited to legal, accounting and other professional
advisory fees, in connection with the transactions contemplated by this letter of intent.
12. 	Public Announcements. None of the Company, the Stockholders or Purchaser will make any
announcement of the proposed transaction contemplated by this letter of intent prior to the execution
of the Purchase Agreement without the prior written approval of the other, which approval will not be
unreasonably withheld or delayed. Purchaser and the Company will jointly announce the transaction
after the Purchase Agreement has been executed. The foregoing shall not restrict in any respect the
Company’s, any Stockholder’s or Purchaser’s ability to communicate information concerning this
letter of intent and the transactions contemplated hereby to their respective affiliates and its and their
respective officers, directors, employees and professional advisers, and, to the extent relevant, to third
parties whose consent is required in connection with the transaction contemplated by this letter of
intent.
13. 	Exclusive Negotiating Rights. In order to induce YYYY to commit the resources, forego other
potential opportunities, and incur the legal, accounting, and incidental expenses necessary properly to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

evaluate the possibility of acquiring the Equity Interests as described above, the Company agrees that
from the date of this letter of intent through _______________, none of the Company, the
Stockholders, their affiliates or any of their respective officers, directors, employees and agents shall
initiate, solicit, encourage, directly or indirectly, or accept any offer or proposal, regarding the possible
acquisition by any person or entity other than Purchaser, including, without limitation, by way of a
purchase of shares, purchase of assets or merger, of all or any substantial part of the Company’s equity
securities or assets, and shall not (other than in the ordinary course of business as heretofore conducted)
provide any confidential information regarding the Company’s assets or business to any person other
than Purchaser and Purchaser’s representatives.
14. 	Next Steps. After the parties execute this letter of intent, the next steps would be for Purchaser
to immediately initiate its due diligence review while concurrently commencing work on the
preparation of a Purchase Agreement, the initial draft of which will be prepared by YYYY’s counsel.
The general terms and conditions of the transaction as defined in this document would form the basis
for such Purchase Agreement. Issues (if any) uncovered during the due diligence review would be
negotiated and addressed within the Purchase Agreement. The Purchase Agreement will be governed
by Delaware law.
15. 	Binding Effect. Except for paragraphs 6, 7, 8, 11 through 14 and 16 through 18 (the “Binding
Provisions”), this letter of intent does not constitute a binding agreement among the parties but merely
expresses their intent with respect to the transactions contemplated hereby. A binding agreement
regarding the transactions contemplated hereby will be created only upon the execution and delivery
of the Purchase Agreement by the parties thereto. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the obligations of
the parties pursuant to the Binding Provisions are intended to be binding and enforceable obligations.
The Binding Provisions shall survive any termination of this letter of intent, and shall be binding upon
and inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns.
16. 	Miscellaneous. This letter shall be governed by the substantive laws of the State of
Aaaa without regard to conflict of law principles. This letter constitutes the entire understanding and
agreement between the parties hereto and their affiliates with respect to its subject matter and
supersedes all prior or contemporaneous agreements, representations, warranties and understandings
of such parties (whether oral or written). No promise, inducement, representation or agreement, other
than as expressly set forth herein, has been made to or by the parties hereto. This letter may be amended
only by written agreement, signed by the parties to be bound by the amendment. This letter shall be
construed according to its fair meaning and not strictly for or against either party.
